title: Unveiling the power of code pre-trained models in automated program repair: A systematic review
data:
S Zhan, X Wang, D Wei, X Xiang, Y Lin, H Wu, J Lu- Journal of Systems and Software, 2026
Abstract Automated Program Repair (APR) aims to automatically fix software bugs, 
thereby enhancing software reliability. In recent years, Code Pre-trained Models 
(CodePTMs) have attracted considerable attention in the APR domain. While recent 
surveys cover the application of LLMs in SE broadly, there is a lack of focused 
analysis on the architectural evolution and specific integration strategies of

title: Discrepancy-Aware GraphLanguage Modelling for Cross-Repository Vulnerability Intelligence Alignment and Automated Repair
data:
L Wang - 2026
Software vulnerability management relies on accurate, coherent vulnerability 
intelligence and on practical support for localising and repairing vulnerable code. In 
reality, this intelligence is scattered across heterogeneous repositories (eg, NVD, 
CERT, vendor advisories, community trackers), written in unstructured text and often 
inconsistent or incomplete, which hampers risk assessment, patch prioritisation and 
the effectiveness of analysis and repair tools. This thesis investigates how such
